reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures the informal claim. `[Infinite F] [Field F]` matches "infinite field", `[AddCommGroup V] [Module F V]` matches "vector space over F", and `[Finite ι]` with `u : ι → Submodule F V` encodes "a finite number of subspaces" (indexing by a finite type; possible duplicates don't affect the union). The hypothesis `u i ≠ ⊤` is precisely "proper subspace" (not equal to the whole space). The conclusion `(⋃ i, (u i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` says the union of the underlying sets is not all of V, since `⊤ : Set V` is `univ`. Edge cases (empty ι, trivial V) are handled consistently with the informal reading since V always contains 0 so is nonempty. No hidden extra hypotheses or omitted ones.
